text.title: AIO Risk Scoring Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that make AIO risk scoring an executable decision tool, not a discussion topic.

rule.statement: Every priority query MUST be scored on SIX axes, each on a 0-4 integer scale: intent_class, panel_persistence, snippet_length, monetisation_potential, our_citation_eligibility, alternative_value. The risk_index is the weighted sum (default weights documented in `scoring-rubric.json`). Free-form scoring without the six axes is REJECTED.
rule.rationale: A single-number "AIO risk" hides the trade-offs. The six axes force the marketer to articulate what kind of risk: high intent + high panel persistence is different from low monetisation + high citation eligibility. Each axis maps to a specific countermeasure.

rule.statement: The risk_index maps to one of three actions: `skip` (don't write), `target` (write only for citation hunting), `hedge` (write but invest in middle-funnel CTAs). The mapping uses thresholds documented in scoring-rubric.json. Ad-hoc actions outside this set MUST update the rubric before becoming routine.
rule.rationale: Typed decisions accumulate institutional learning. Off-rubric actions produce one-off bets that don't compound. The three-action set is small enough to be remembered and broad enough to cover real cases.

rule.statement: Every priority query MUST be re-scored at least quarterly OR when its tracker presence shifts by > 25 points. Scores are NOT permanent; AIO behaviour shifts as Google retunes the panel. Decisions older than 6 months without re-evaluation are REJECTED.
rule.rationale: AIO selection criteria change with Google retunes. Quarterly re-score catches drift; threshold-triggered re-score catches sudden shifts. Static decisions are a known anti-pattern.

rule.statement: The monetisation_potential axis MUST derive from actual analytics — estimated revenue per click per query from the team's analytics model. Gut-feel "this query is valuable" is REJECTED. Queries with monetisation_potential = 0 default to `skip` regardless of citation eligibility (citation alone, without business impact, is vanity).
rule.rationale: Citation rate is an output metric; revenue is the outcome metric. Acting on citation without revenue tracking is the dominant SEO failure mode. Tying the rubric to revenue keeps it honest.

rule.statement: Every decision row MUST include a `rationale` field with at least 60 characters explaining the decision. The rationale references specific axis scores and / or recent tracker observations. Empty / generic rationale ("low ROI") is REJECTED.
rule.rationale: Rationale fields make future re-scores tractable. The next quarter's reviewer can see why the prior decision was made and challenge it on specifics. Empty rationale forces the next reviewer to start from scratch.

section.title: The six axes (summary)

list.item: panel_persistence (0-4): how stable is AIO panel presence on this query (4=present in >80% of scrapes).
snippet_length (0-4): AIO snippet length vs query answer completeness (4=AIO answers fully, 0=AIO truncates).
monetisation_potential (0-4): inverted axis — 4=high revenue per click (lowers net risk).
our_citation_eligibility (0-4): inverted axis — 4=we are already cited / highly eligible.
alternative_value (0-4): inverted axis — 4=middle-funnel content can capture meaningful value via CTAs.

section.reference: Full rubric: axis definitions, scoring questions, threshold table.

example.title: Skip decision on zero-click query with low monetisation
example.code: Query: "what time is it in Tokyo" Scores: intent_class=4 (pure info), panel_persistence=4, snippet_length=4 (fully answered), monetisation_potential=0, our_citation_eligibility=2, alternative_value=0. Risk index: 18 / 24 → skip. Rationale: "Pure informational query with full panel answer and zero monetisation; no CTA path; writing wastes effort. Re-evaluate Q2 2027."
example.note: r1, r4, r5 satisfied. The decision is documented; rationale is specific to axis scores.

example.title: Single-number ad-hoc scoring
example.code: Query: "best CRM for solos" Score: 7/10 (gut feel). Decision: write. Rationale: "feels important"
example.note: r1 + r5 violations. No axis breakdown; rationale empty. The next reviewer cannot challenge or update.
